notes: Fifteenth validated clue batch compiled from official school websites.
Clues intentionally avoid revealing enrollment size, grade span, or directional/proximity information because those are gameplay feedback channels.

clues.text: Its mission says students should master the academic basics while developing principled centered lives and an appreciation for the dignity of work.
Official materials say the school opened in 1999.
The school says it is part of the National Heritage Academies network and integrates Moral Focus lessons into each school day.
A school blog says it received the National School Choice Week Leadership Award.
Its leadership page lists Carmen Palmer as board president.
Official pages list Christy Harnsberger as principal.
Its mission says the school works in partnership with parents and the community to offer a challenging, character-based education in an atmosphere of high expectations.
Official materials say the school opened in 2000.
The school says it is part of National Heritage Academies and emphasizes Moral Focus as part of daily instruction.
Its public pages highlight a structured setting built on clear expectations and consistent routines.
Its leadership page lists Sheila Corbett as board president.
Official pages list Shemeka Dixon as principal.
Its mission says the school was created to serve children in southeastern Durham and give working parents near Research Triangle Park a quality school close to the workplace.
Official materials say the school opened in 2000.
A parent and student handbook includes a student creed focused on academic excellence, high moral character, realizing potential, and working diligently to prepare for the future.
The school says more than four out of five families report a positive impact from its Moral Focus program.
Its leadership page lists Elizabeth Gnatek-Morey as board president.
Official pages list Patrice Hardy as principal.
Official materials say the school has focused on quality education since 1999.
Its overview says four defining elements are protected learning environments, direct instruction, highest quality teaching staff, and high expectations.
Admissions materials say the school was named in the national top twenty schools in the Classic Learning Test's 2022-2023 CLT8 rankings.
Achievements pages say the school has won multiple state archery championships and national cheerleading titles.
Network governance pages list Colleen Combs as chair of the Board of Trustees.
Campus pages list Laurie Benton as headmaster.
Official materials say the school was established in July 2014 as a replicate campus modeled on the Leland and Whiteville schools.
Its overview says four defining elements are protected learning environments, direct instruction, highest quality teaching staff, and high expectations.
The school says it is a premier performance-driven year-round school.
Network overview pages say Classical Charter Schools of America features classical literature, chronological Western history, cursive handwriting, and Latin in its curriculum.
Network governance pages list Colleen Combs as chair of the Board of Trustees.
Campus pages list Amanda Sauls as headmaster.
